_comment: Flow — KVRB Vero Beach part-time tower: apt.dat lists 126.300 as BOTH TOWER and UNICOM because when the tower is closed the same freq becomes CTAF/UNICOM. Bug prior to 2026-04-24: AirportFrequencies::lookup() returned the first insertion-order match (UNICOM), breaking the Ground->Tower handoff for takeoff. Fix: lookup() now resolves ties by priority (TOWER > GROUND > ... > UNICOM). This scenario uses the 'freqs' field so lookup() is exercised end-to-end (not via hardcoded freq_type).
name: KVRB part-time tower — Ground->Tower handoff on shared 126.300

context.callsign: November One Two Three Alpha Bravo

say.note: Sanity: on 127.450 lookup() must resolve to GROUND.
say.text: Vero Beach Ground November One Two Three Alpha Bravo at the ramp ready to taxi

say.note: Still on Ground: ready for departure — expect 'contact Tower' handoff.
say.text: Vero Beach Ground November One Two Three Alpha Bravo holding short ready for departure

say.note: Switched to 126.300. Bug precondition: apt.dat lists UNICOM before TOWER, so broken lookup() would return UNICOM here and the next tower call would be rejected by the frequency guard. Priority fix must yield TOWER.
say.text: Vero Beach Tower November One Two Three Alpha Bravo ready for departure

say.note: Pattern on Tower 126.300 — still TOWER after airborne switch.
say.text: November One Two Three Alpha Bravo midfield left downwind
November One Two Three Alpha Bravo on final full stop
